title: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser
impact: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser.

updates.content:
Title: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser

User impact: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser.

More info: The managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser may be displaying an error stating “Action Not Allowed” when trying to launch the Microsoft Intune web app.
 
Current status: This is a continuation of IT1281088. We're continuing to creating a new build of the Microsoft Edge iOS browser that has the account management feature disabled to remediate impact. 
 
Scope of impact: Any user attempting to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser may be impacted.
 
Root cause: A code issue in the Mobile Application Management (MAM) SDK conflicts with a recently enabled account management feature, resulting in impact.
 
Next update by: Wednesday, April 15, 2026, at 9:00 PM UTC

Title: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser

User impact: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser.

More info: The managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser may be displaying an error stating “Action Not Allowed” when trying to launch the Microsoft Intune web app.
This only impacts those with iOS webapps created and targeted to users, and configured to launch in managed Microsoft Edge browser.
 
Current status: We're testing a new build of the Microsoft Edge iOS browser that has the account management feature disabled to remediate impact. Once testing is complete, we'll determine the timeline for release and provide the version number.
 
Scope of impact: Any user who's attempting to access the created and targeted iOS Microsoft Intune web app that's configured to launch in a managed Microsoft Edge browser may be affected.
 
Root cause: A code issue in the Mobile Application Management (MAM) SDK conflicts with a recently enabled account management feature, resulting in impact.
 
Next update by: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 3:00 AM UTC
